summary.intent: Add lightweight model support so basic tasks (like doc summarization) can use cheaper/faster models instead of the full model, with each AI provider declaring its own lightweight model string.
summary.outcome: Successfully implemented lightweight model support across 14 files: new `lightweightModel` property on all 5 providers, `model` option in `IProviderInvokeOptions`, `useLightweightModel` flag in `IIsolatedPromptOptions`, docs-finder updated to use it, and all 6 mock provider test files updated. All 1324 tests pass, lint and typecheck clean, committed as `0f9bd26`.

repo: Pre-commit hook runs `bun test src/` — it scans the entire `src/` tree, but also risks picking up unrelated test files if leftover cloned repos exist under `tmp/clones/`.

6 test files contain inline mock providers implementing `IAIProvider` that must be kept in sync with interface changes: isolated-prompt.test.ts, docs-finder.test.ts, ai-file-consolidator.test.ts, prompt-generator.test.ts (no mock), registry.test.ts, semantic-deduplicator.test.ts.
OpenCode provider has special `--variant medium` flag logic that should only apply to the default model and must be skipped when using a non-default/lightweight model.
The project uses Biome for linting and Bun for test running and typechecking.
Working tree contained 8 pre-existing unrelated modified files; only the 14 task-related files were staged and committed.

code.finding: Both `IProviderInvokeOptions` (add `model?: string`) and `IAIProvider` (add `readonly lightweightModel: string`) live in this file — the central contract for all provider changes.
`--variant medium` is a default-model-only flag; it must be conditionally skipped when `model` is set to a non-default value, otherwise the CLI call mixes incompatible flags.
Model resolution priority in `invokeIsolated()`: explicit `options.model` > `useLightweightModel ? provider.lightweightModel : undefined` > provider default (undefined).
`summarizeDocFile()` is the only current call site for `useLightweightModel: true`; it generates single-sentence summaries that don't require a powerful model.

workflow: When a pre-commit hook runs the test suite, leftover directories from cloned repos (`tmp/clones/`) can cause spurious test failures — clean them up before committing.
Staging only task-related files (`git add <explicit paths>`) keeps commits focused when the working tree has unrelated in-progress changes.
Reading all affected files in parallel at the start of an implementation session saves multiple round-trips and gives a complete picture before making any edits.
After adding a required interface property, run typecheck immediately to catch all mock providers that missed the update rather than discovering them test-by-test.

friction: Pre-commit hook failed on first commit attempt because `tmp/clones/` contained a leftover cloned repository whose test files were picked up by `bun test src/` — required manual cleanup of `tmp/clones/` before the commit succeeded.
Two mock providers (semantic-deduplicator.test.ts and ai-file-consolidator.test.ts) were missed in the initial mock update pass and required a second typecheck round to catch.

GitHub Copilot provider required three separate Edit operations due to incremental additions (lightweightModel property, then invoke model arg, then a fix), suggesting the initial edit was incomplete.
open_items: 8 pre-existing modified files remain uncommitted in the working tree — these are unrelated changes that were intentionally excluded from this commit.
No other callers currently use `useLightweightModel: true` besides `summarizeDocFile()` — other simple/cheap invocation sites could be audited and opted in to reduce cost further.
